tweets.text:
7⃣ WORLDS INVITES

The number of invites to the 🗺️ 2027 World Championships in TPCi regions has been increased! 📈
▶️ Regional & SC Champions
▶️ Top 4 at Internationals
▶️ Top players in the TPCi leaderboards according to this table 🔽

5⃣ ONLINE EVENTS

The structure of Global Challenges and Grand Challenges is maintained 🟰

Global Challenges have been announced for September, October, December, January and March, and a Grand Challenge in April! 🗓️

4⃣ MAJOR EVENTS

Regional, Special and International Championships will award some more points in the lower placements (Top 512 to 1,024) 📈

Travel Awards (starting with EUIC) will have CP tiebreakers depending on the highest placements at tournaments 👥

3⃣ LOCAL EVENTS

League Challenges and League Cups are kept unchanged from 2026 🟰

The CP-awarding side events in majors will now be named Pokémon Challenges and Pokémon Cups, with different prizing but same CP and BFL as the League Challenges & Cups 🧑‍🏫

2⃣ AGE RESTRICTIONS

Due to the age classifications of #PokemonChampions, younger competitors will NOT be allowed in tournaments in some regions 🧒

🇩🇪 Germany: no players under 12
🇧🇷 Brazil: no players under 14

Alternative solutions for younger competitors are being explored.

1⃣ ALLOWED DEVICES

All tournaments (both locals and majors) will allow any Nintendo Switch or Nintendo Switch 2 consoles 🎮

Mobiles will not be allowed for official in-person tournaments 🚫

The 🗺️ 2027 World Championships will have a different policy, still to be announced 🆕
